title: lathyrus polymorphus var. incanus
intro: Lathyrus polymorphus var. incanus, also known as the hairy vetchling, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. It is a perennial herb with a history of use in traditional medicine, where its seeds and flowers have been employed for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus polymorphus var. incanus are thought to be attributed to its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus polymorphus var. incanus can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
